description: Calculates the distribution of users based on their latest balance points as of a specific date. Users are grouped into predefined point ranges (1-50, 51-150, 151-1,000, 1,001+) and the percentage of total users in each range is returned.
usage: To use this query, run it against the postgres database using the postgres-query skill. You may need to update the 'balance_point_timestamp' in the WHERE clause (e.g., '2026-03-31 23:59:59') to reflect the desired snapshot date.
sql:
WITH latest_points AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        balance_point
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            user_id, 
            balance_point,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER(
                PARTITION BY user_id 
                ORDER BY balance_point_timestamp DESC, point_id DESC 
            ) as rn
        FROM tulip_crm_prod.public.member_transaction_point6
        WHERE balance_point_timestamp <= '2026-03-31 23:59:59'
    ) t
    WHERE rn = 1 
      AND balance_point > 0 
),
summary_data AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN balance_point >= 1 AND balance_point <= 50 THEN '1 - 50'
            WHEN balance_point > 50 AND balance_point <= 150 THEN '51 - 150'
            WHEN balance_point > 150 AND balance_point <= 1000 THEN '151 - 1,000'
            WHEN balance_point > 1000 THEN '1,001+' 
            ELSE '0 Points / Others' 
        END AS point_range,
        COUNT(user_id) AS total_users
    FROM latest_points
    GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT 
    point_range,
    total_users,
    ROUND(total_users * 100.0 / SUM(total_users) OVER(), 2) AS percentage
FROM summary_data
ORDER BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN point_range = '1 - 50' THEN 1
        WHEN point_range = '51 - 150' THEN 2
        WHEN point_range = '151 - 1,000' THEN 3
        WHEN point_range = '1,001+' THEN 4
        ELSE 5 
    END;
